name: spec2integration
description: In-IDE project launcher, navigator and guided command composer for the Spec2Integration spec-driven integration pipeline. Scaffolds greenfield or BizTalk-migration workspaces (bundled agents/skills/commands), shows every integration's pipeline stages live from status.json, and hands assembled slash commands to your Claude Code chat.

viewsWelcome.contents:
Start a new integration project.

The extension scaffolds a workspace pre-loaded with the right agents, skills and commands for your path, then opens it ready for Claude Code.
[$(sparkle) Start a Greenfield integration](command:spec2integration.newProject?%5B%22greenfield%22%5D)
[$(history) Migrate from BizTalk](command:spec2integration.newProject?%5B%22biztalk%22%5D)

Already have a project? Open its folder to see its pipeline here.
[Refresh bundled assets from GitHub](command:spec2integration.updateAssets)

Migrate a BizTalk solution.

**Step 1** — tell the extension where your BizTalk solution lives: the folder containing the `.btproj` files (or an MSI export). It can be anywhere on disk.
[$(folder-opened) Specify BizTalk Source Folder](command:spec2integration.setBiztalkSource)

Once set, this becomes **Run Pipeline** — which inventories the solution, authors the spec and contracts, compiles the IR, and runs through to tests.

BizTalk source set. ✓

**Step 2 — Analyse.** Catalog the solution (inventory + catalogue). Each integration group then appears under the BizTalk node, where **Run Pipeline** migrates the one you pick (or All), with option checkboxes (unattended, auto-fix…).
[$(search) Analyse Solution](command:spec2integration.analyseSolution)

[$(folder-opened) Change source folder](command:spec2integration.setBiztalkSource) · [Compose a command](command:spec2integration.composeCommand)

The BizTalk node and its groups appear here once analysis writes the catalogue under `specs/biztalk/`.

No integrations found yet.

Run the pipeline to create your first integration — the wizard collects options, then sends the assembled slash command to your Claude Code chat.
[$(run-all) Run Pipeline](command:spec2integration.runFullPipeline)
[$(zap) Compose a command](command:spec2integration.composeCommand)

As the pipeline produces `status.json` files under `specs/`, integrations appear here automatically — this panel refreshes the moment the first one lands.

enumDescriptions: Open a new Claude Code conversation pre-filled with the assembled command via the extension's in-process command (no OS round-trip). Falls back to the deep link, then the clipboard, if the command is unavailable.
Open the Claude Code chat pre-filled with the assembled command via a vscode:// deep link. Falls back to the clipboard if the link is rejected.
Copy the assembled command to the clipboard and show a notification to paste it into chat.
spec2integration.chatDelivery.description: How an assembled slash command is handed to Claude Code. 'command' is the most reliable (direct in-process call); switch to 'deeplink' or 'clipboard' if a future Claude Code version renames its internal command. Note: all modes open a NEW conversation — Claude Code does not allow injecting into an already-open session.

spec2integration.deepLinkScheme.description: The vscode:// deep-link target used to open the Claude Code chat with a prompt. The assembled command is appended as ?prompt=<encoded>. Adjust if your Claude Code extension uses a different URI.

spec2integration.previewBeforeSend.description: Before delivering an assembled command, show it in an editable box for review. Turn off to send immediately.

spec2integration.continueSession.description: Keep an entire pipeline run in a single Claude Code conversation tab instead of opening a new one per command. The first command mints a reusable session via one tool-free headless `claude -p` call (no permission prompts); later commands resume it via the documented `session=` deep link and refocus its tab. Requires the `claude` CLI on PATH and signed in; falls back to a new conversation if a session can't be established. Use 'Reset Pipeline Chat Session' to start a fresh one.

spec2integration.claudeCliPath.description: Path to the Claude Code CLI used to mint a reusable session when `continueSession` is enabled. Override if `claude` is not on your PATH.

walkthroughs.title: Greenfield: brief → deployable integration
walkthroughs.description: Drive a rough brief to a deployable integration through the guided pipeline.

steps.title: Draft the PRD
steps.description:
Turn your brief into a structured PRD. The new project opened with this step ready to send.
[Draft PRD](command:spec2integration.composeCommand)

steps.title: Specify → model → contracts → map → architect
steps.description:
Advance one stage at a time, or let /run-pipeline orchestrate the whole flow. Each stage records its result in status.json.
[Compose a command](command:spec2integration.composeCommand)

steps.title: Advance the pipeline
steps.description:
Each integration records its recommended next step. Run it from the tree, the status bar, or the command palette.
[Run Next Step](command:spec2integration.runNext)

steps.title: Watch progress live
steps.description:
Open the progress cockpit for a live stage table, coverage and timings; stage-completion and Sev-1 toasts surface as the pipeline runs.
[Open Pipeline Progress](command:spec2integration.openProgress)

steps.title: Visualize the IR
steps.description:
Open the integration-ir.yaml as an interactive flow graph.
[Open IR Visualizer](command:spec2integration.openVisualizer)

walkthroughs.title: BizTalk: migrate to Azure Integration Services
walkthroughs.description: Analyse a BizTalk solution, then migrate each integration group to Azure one at a time.

steps.title: Specify the BizTalk source
steps.description:
Point the extension at your BizTalk solution — the folder with the `.btproj` files, or an MSI export. It can live anywhere on disk; your choice is remembered.
[Specify BizTalk Source Folder](command:spec2integration.setBiztalkSource)

steps.title: Analyse the solution
steps.description:
Catalog every orchestration, map, schema, pipeline and binding, scored by migration complexity, and group them into `INT-NNN` integration boundaries. Each group then appears under the BizTalk node.
[Analyse Solution](command:spec2integration.analyseSolution)

steps.title: Migrate a group
steps.description:
Run Pipeline lets you pick **one** integration group (or All) and toggle options as checkboxes (unattended, auto-fix, …). It authors the spec, contracts and IR for that group and runs through to tests — each group becomes its own integration. Re-run for the next group.
[Run Pipeline](command:spec2integration.runFullPipeline)

steps.title: Watch progress live
steps.description:
The progress cockpit shows the stage table, generated artifacts, blocked flows (custom code needing attention) and coverage; Sev-1 toasts flag constitution blockers.
[Open Pipeline Progress](command:spec2integration.openProgress)

steps.title: Visualize the IR
steps.description:
Open the integration-ir.yaml as an interactive flow graph, with migration findings overlaid on the nodes.
[Open IR Visualizer](command:spec2integration.openVisualizer)

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run sync-assets && npm run bundle
